questions.question: What is the '400-Person Rule' mentioned in the lesson?

options.text: You must have 400 lines of code before launching.
You should have an audience of at least 400 people ready to support your launch.
You must get 400 upvotes to get a badge.
The launch costs $400.

questions.explanation: Product Hunt amplifies existing momentum. Without an 'initial push' from a predictable audience, the algorithm will likely bury your post.

questions.question: Why is it recommended to use an animated GIF for your product thumbnail?

options.text: It makes the page load faster.
It's the only way to stand out in a feed of static images.
Product Hunt requires it for all posts.
It helps with SEO.

questions.explanation: Visual competition on PH is fierce. Movement catches the eye and increases the click-through rate to your product page.

questions.question: At what time (Pacific Time) should you ideally launch your Product Hunt post?

options.text: 9:00 AM PT
12:01 AM PT
5:00 PM PT
Noon PT

questions.explanation: Launching right at the start of the daily window (12:01 AM PT) gives you the full 24 hours to accumulate upvotes and engagement.

questions.question: Which of these behaviors is most likely to result in a 'Shadow-Ban' or penalty on Product Hunt?

options.text: Responding to every comment personally.
Directly asking for upvotes and using 'Upvote Pods' to manipulate the algorithm.
Launching on a Tuesday.
Offering a discount code in your Maker Comment.

questions.explanation: Product Hunt has advanced detection for manipulation. Genuinely asking for 'feedback' or 'support' is safe; transactional upvoting is not.

questions.question: What is the most important 'Success Metric' for a solo founder on PH, according to the lesson?

options.text: Total number of upvotes.
Reaching the #1 spot.
New trials, sign-ups, and the ability to convert commenters into conversations.
Getting a tweet from the PH founder.

questions.explanation: Vanity metrics like upvotes are nice, but business momentum comes from actual users and the relationships you build with people who engaged with your 'milestone'.

questions.question: If you were to launch on Product Hunt 30 days from now, what is your specific 'Audience Gap'? (e.g., 'I only have 50 email subscribers, I need 350 more'). List 3 ways you will bridge that gap using the strategies from Lessons 1-4 (Reddit, Twitter, etc.).
promptHints: Consider how 'Building in Public' can help grow your warmup list.
Think about 'Borrowing' trust from other communities to build your own list.
